appliedUpdates.description: Added .factory/library/nats-config-syntax.md documenting NATS config array element separator behavior (newline-separated vs comma-separated). Workers writing test configs with inline arrays encountered parsing issues that required multiple edits.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on ambiguous promoted field behavior: when multiple embedded structs provide the same field name, the v2 Unmarshal uses last-write-wins semantics rather than encoding/json's inaccessibility approach. Document this as intentional for config parsing simplicity, or align with encoding/json if strict compatibility is desired.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: unmarshal-core review: conf/v2/unmarshal.go buildFieldIndexRecursive() at line 150 uses last-write-wins for ambiguous promoted fields. Three related non-blocking issues were found around encoding/json behavioral differences (ambiguous promoted fields, 32-bit overflow, unexported anonymous struct promotion).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reinforce TDD procedure enforcement in go-library-worker skill. The unmarshal-complex-types worker created implementation before tests despite the skill mandating tests-first. Consider adding explicit checkpoint reminders or requiring test file creation as the first step before any implementation file.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: unmarshal-complex-types review: transcript shows unmarshal.go (implementation) was created BEFORE unmarshal_complex_test.go (tests), contradicting the skill's step 3 'Write Tests First (TDD - MANDATORY)'. Worker self-reported followedProcedure: true in handoff, which was inaccurate.
